LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS YOSEF PERETZ AND SUMY KIM'S MOTION FOR ANTI-SLAPP ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED. MR. PERETZ AND MS. KIM ARE ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $16,440 AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,242.24 AGAINST PLAINTIFF KAZIM ACAR. AS PREVAILING PARTIES ON AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION, MR. PERETZ AND MS. KIM ARE ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF THEIR REASONABLY INCURRED FEES AND COSTS. THEY PROVIDED AMPLE EVIDENCE THAT THE AMOUNTS THEY SEEK WERE REASONABLY INCURRED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Sumy Kim of the law offices of Peretz & Associates (415.732.3777, ext. 105) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants Yosef Peretz and Sumy Kim's motion for anti-SLAPP attorney's fees and costs is GRANTED. Mr. Peretz and Ms. Kim are entitled to an award of fees in the amount of $16,440 and costs in the amount of $1,242.24 against plaintiff Kazim Acar. As prevailing parties on an anti-SLAPP motion, Mr. Peretz and Ms. Kim are entitled to an award of their reasonably incurred fees and costs. They provided ample evidence that the amounts they seek were reasonably incurred. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike The Complaint As A Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation
Off Calendar Mar-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT JENNIFER CRANE, FAHIMEH RAHRAVAN Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike The Complaint As A Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation is off calendar as a duplicate entry. (302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defendants Yosef Peretzs And Sumy Kims Special Motion To Strike The Complaint As A Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation
Off Calendar Mar-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff KAZIM ACAR, Tel No. 415-912-6800 appearing in pro per;
Atty. YOSEF PERETZ, ESQ. of Peretz & Associates, Tel No. 415-732-3777 appearing for Defendants Jennifer Crane and Fahimeh Rahravan;
Atty. DANIEL CRAVENS, ESQ. of Cravens & Associates, Tel No. 559-472-6584 appearing for Defendants Yosef Peretz and Sumy Kim.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS YOSEF PERETZ AND SUMY KIM'S anti-SLAPP motion to strike plaintiff's first amended complaint is granted in its entirety. Defendants have satisfied their burden of showing that all of plaintiff's claims are based on activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. All of plaintiff's claims are based on Peretz and Kim's representation of Crane and Rahravan in litigation and thus all of those claims are based on protected petitioning activities per Code of Civil Procedure 425.16. Plaintiff has not satisfied his burden of showing that any of his claims have the requisite minimal merit to avoid dismissal. Plaintiff's slander claim lacks minimal merit because statements that defendants made in the course of the underlying lawsuits are protected under Civil Code section 47(b). (Rusheen v. Cohen (2006) 37 Cal.4th 1048, 1062.) Plaintiff's malicious prosecution claim lacks minimal merit because none of the underlying claims ended in Plaintiff's favor. (Siebel v. Mittlesteadt (2007) 41 Cal.4th 735, 740.) Plaintiff's claims for filing frivolous motions, intentional misrepresentation of facts, and civil conspiracy lack minimal merit because they are not cognizable. (Berg & Berg Enterprises, LLC v. Sherwood Partners, Inc. (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 802, 823.) Plaintiff's claim for breach of contract lacks minimal merit because plaintiff and Peretz never had a contract that could be breached. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEFENDANTS JENNIFER CRANE And FAHIMEH RAHRAVAN'S Special Motion To Strike The First Amended Complaint As A Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation is granted in its entirety. Defendants have satisfied their burden of showing that all of plaintiff's claims are based on activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. All of plaintiff's claims are based on Peretz and Kim's representation of Crane and Rahravan in litigation and thus all of those claims are based on protected petitioning activities per Code of Civil Procedure 425.16. Plaintiff has not satisfied his burden of showing that any of his claims have the requisite minimal merit to avoid dismissal. Plaintiff's slander claim lacks minimal merit because statements that defendants made in the course of the underlying lawsuits are protected under Civil Code section 47(b). (Rusheen v. Cohen (2006) 37 Cal.4th 1048, 1062.) Plaintiff's malicious prosecution claim lacks minimal merit because none of the underlying claims ended in Plaintiff's favor. (Siebel v. Mittlesteadt (2007) 41 Cal.4th 735, 740.) Plaintiff's claims for filing frivolous motions, intentional misrepresentation of facts, and civil conspiracy lack minimal merit because they are not cognizable. (Berg & Berg Enterprises, LLC v. Sherwood Partners, Inc. (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 802, 823.) Plaintiff's claim for breach of contract lacks minimal merit because plaintiff and Peretz never had a contract that could be breached. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
